description:
Grounding from current code:
- `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs` still fabricates `legacy_extern_ty`, `legacy_extern_contract`, and a fresh legacy type owner only to populate `fz_ir::ExternDecl.ret_descr` / `semantic_contract`.
- The compiler2-native backend no longer uses those fields as authority after `NativeProgram(root)`; they remain because the shared fz-IR/native substrate still carries old-world extern semantic payloads on `ExternDecl`.

Goal: make the compiler2/native extern handoff truthful by construction. Signal: compiler2-native no longer needs to mint legacy extern semantic payloads just to satisfy `fz_ir::ExternDecl`; the runtime/codegen substrate consumes an extern surface that carries only backend-consumption facts.

Change:
- identify every backend/runtime consumer that still requires `ExternDecl.ret_descr` or `semantic_contract`
- delete those old-world payloads from the compiler2/native handoff, or split the shared extern surface so compiler2-native does not have to carry them
- remove the temporary legacy extern projection helpers from `src/compiler2/jobs/native.rs` once the substrate no longer needs them

Why:
- `fz-rh2.14` closed the big backend ownership cut, but this is the remaining contained pocket where compiler2-native still manufactures old-world semantic data it does not semantically own
title: Compiler2 native substrate: remove legacy extern semantic payloads from ExternDecl
